• **Formal Statement**: The total vector potential in a gyromagnetic system comprises contributions: \(A_{\text{total}}(r,t) = A_{\text{dipole}} + A_{\text{gyro}} + A_{\text{induced}}\), where \(A_{\text{dipole}} = (\mu_0/4\pi) \mu_{\text{eff}}(t) \times (r - r_{\text{cm}})/|r-r_{\text{cm}}|^3\), \(A_{\text{gyro}} = (\mu_0/4\pi) \int_{V_{\text{gyro}}} J_{\text{gyro}}(r',t) \times (r - r')/|r - r'|^3 \; d^3r'\), and \(A_{\text{induced}} = -(1/4\pi c^2) \int_V (\partial J/\partial t) \times (r - r')/|r - r'| \; d^3r'\)[374768018451485 L69-L93].
